column1: 48 Vows of Amitābha Buddha as found in the Larger Sukhāvatīvyūha Sūtra. Each vow is condensed for clarity and easy reference.

column3: Core Promise / Condition

column2: No Hell, Hungry Ghosts, or Animals
column3: No beings in Pure Land will be reborn in hell, as hungry ghosts, or animals.
No beings in Pure Land will fall into the three evil realms.
All beings in Pure Land have bodies of genuine gold color.
All beings have equal appearance, no ugliness or nobility.
All beings remember their past lives.
All beings possess the divine eye (clairvoyance).
All beings possess the divine ear (clairaudience).
All beings know the thoughts of others.
All beings know the past lives of others.
Women, after hearing Amitābha’s name, renounce female form and become men.
No beings have evil thoughts or commit evil acts.
Amitābha’s light is infinite and illuminates all worlds.
Amitābha’s life is infinite.
All beings in the ten directions hear Amitābha’s name.
All beings can be reborn in Pure Land at will.
All beings attain the stage of non-retrogression.
All beings in Pure Land will become Buddhas.
Those who sincerely aspire to be reborn in Pure Land will be reborn there.
Those who plant roots of virtue and aspire to be reborn will be welcomed by Amitābha.
Those who dedicate merit to rebirth in Pure Land will be reborn there.
All beings see Amitābha at the moment of death.
All beings attain equal samādhi and reach Buddhahood quickly.
All beings possess eloquence and wisdom.
All beings can preach Dharma everywhere.
All beings can make offerings to all Buddhas.
All beings possess supernatural powers.
All beings are adorned with virtue.
Pure Land abounds with heavenly music and fragrance.
Trees and jewels in Pure Land radiate light and sound Dharma.
Flowers rain down from the sky, delighting all beings.
No beings have attachment or defilement.
No suffering, only joy, in Pure Land.
No darkness or night in Pure Land.
No extremes of heat or cold in Pure Land.
No beings experience hunger or thirst.
Clothing and ornaments appear spontaneously.
All wishes are instantly fulfilled.
No beings suffer aging or sickness.
No gender distinctions among beings.
No slander, quarrels, or disputes.
No poverty or want in Pure Land.
No evil paths exist in Pure Land.
No beings hold false or heretical views.
No envy or jealousy among beings.
No laziness; all beings diligently cultivate virtue.
No short lifespans; all beings live as long as Amitābha.
No fear or anxiety in Pure Land.
All beings who sincerely call upon Amitābha will be saved and reborn in Pure Land.

column1: Each vow is a summary; the original sutra contains more detailed descriptions.
Some vows overlap in theme (e.g., absence of suffering, universal enlightenment).
The 18th vow (“Primal Vow”) is especially central in Pure Land Buddhism: it promises rebirth to anyone who sincerely calls Amitābha’s name.
